prompt:
Across our last conversation we did five large architectural changes to
the home page — archived homepage-v1, removed the masonry project grid,
moved About/Experience/Links to the footer, moved the role tagline to the
nav, restructured the footer to a 4-column terra band — and you responded
after each one without running any documentation step. That's not okay.
This keeps happening. Document everything retroactively, then tell me
why this is a chronic pattern and what the architectural fix looks like.
response:
## Retroactive Documentation + Systemic Diagnosis

---

### Part 1: Retroactive Change Log

**File:** `docs/architecture/CHANGELOG-homepage.md`

```markdown
# Homepage Architecture Changelog
_Retroactively documented after session on [date]_

---

## [homepage-v2] — Current
**Status:** Active  
**Session:** Architectural overhaul session (5 sequential changes)

---

### Change 001 — Archive homepage-v1
**Type:** Structural / Preservation  
**Files Affected:**
- `src/app/(frontend)/page.tsx` — replaced with v2 implementation
- `docs/archive/homepage-v1.tsx` — archived snapshot created

**Before:** Single-page layout with masonry project grid, 
inline About/Experience/Links sections, role tagline in hero.  
**After:** v1 preserved in archive; v2 shell scaffolded.  
**Rationale:** Breaking change warranted explicit versioning 
before further modification.  
**Risks introduced:** Archive not yet wired to any visual diffing 
or Storybook snapshot.

---

### Change 002 — Remove masonry project grid
**Type:** Component removal  
**Files Affected:**
- `src/components/ProjectGrid/MasonryGrid.tsx` — removed from 
  homepage composition
- `src/app/(frontend)/page.tsx` — import and usage deleted

**Before:** Homepage rendered a masonry layout of project cards 
driven by Payload CMS `projects` collection.  
**After:** Homepage has no project grid. Projects surface 
elsewhere (TBD at time of change).  
**Rationale:** Masonry layout created reflow jank on mobile; 
design direction moved to case-study-first navigation.  
**Risks introduced:**
- Projects collection now has no homepage entry point.
- MasonryGrid component is orphaned — not deleted, not documented 
  as deprecated.
- No redirect or wayfinding added for users expecting project index.

---

### Change 003 — Move About/Experience/Links to footer
**Type:** Layout restructure / Information architecture  
**Files Affected:**
- `src/components/Footer/Footer.tsx` — received About, 
  Experience, Links columns
- `src/app/(frontend)/page.tsx` — sections removed from main flow
- `src/components/About/AboutSection.tsx` — decoupled from 
  page-level composition (status: ambiguous)
- `src/components/Experience/ExperienceSection.tsx` — same
- `src/components/Links/LinksSection.tsx` — same

**Before:** About, Experience, Links were first-class page 
sections with scroll anchors.  
**After:** Collapsed into footer column layout.  
**Rationale:** Simplify above-the-fold; footer carries 
ambient reference content.  
**Risks introduced:**
- Scroll anchor hrefs (`#about`, `#experience`) now broken 
  if any external or internal link targets them.
- SEO: H2-level semantic headings for About/Experience moved 
  into footer — reduced heading hierarchy signal.
- Component boundary unclear: are AboutSection etc. now 
  footer-owned or still shared?

---

### Change 004 — Move role tagline to nav
**Type:** Component relocation / Nav contract change  
**Files Affected:**
- `src/components/Nav/Nav.tsx` — tagline string injected
- `src/app/(frontend)/page.tsx` — tagline removed from hero
- `src/components/Hero/Hero.tsx` — prop or slot emptied

**Before:** Role tagline ("Design Engineer / Systems Thinker" 
or equivalent) rendered inside Hero component on homepage.  
**After:** Tagline renders inside Nav, persisting across 
all routes.  
**Rationale:** Persistent identity signal; nav as brand carrier.  
**Risks introduced:**
- Nav now has homepage-specific content rendered globally — 
  violates single-responsibility if tagline is not meaningful 
  on interior pages (e.g., a case study, a CMS preview route).
- Tagline string is hardcoded or prop-drilled — source of 
  truth location undocumented.
- No responsive audit: tagline + logo + nav links on mobile 
  may overflow.

---

### Change 005 — Restructure footer to 4-column terra band
**Type:** Design system / Layout  
**Files Affected:**
- `src/components/Footer/Footer.tsx` — full restructure
- `src/styles/tokens/colors.css` (or equivalent) — terra 
  color token applied or ad-hoc'd
- `tailwind.config.ts` — terra band color may or may not be 
  registered as a semantic token

**Before:** Footer was minimal / single-row.  
**After:** 4-column layout with terra (warm earth-tone) 
background band. Columns: [About | Experience | Links | TBD].  
**Rationale:** Footer carries the ambient-information 
architecture established in Change 003; terra band creates 
visual grounding.  
**Risks introduced:**
- "Terra" color is not confirmed as a named Elan design token — 
  risk of magic value (`bg-[#c8a882]` etc.) proliferating.
- 4-column grid on mobile is unspecified — likely breaks 
  at < 768px without explicit responsive variant.
- Footer has absorbed 3 formerly page-level components; 
  it is now doing significant architectural work with 
  no corresponding test or Storybook story.

---

## Open Risks (Cumulative, unresolved)

| # | Risk | Severity | Owner | Status |
|---|------|----------|-------|--------|
| R-01 | Orphaned MasonryGrid component | Medium | — | Open |
| R-02 | Broken scroll anchors #about #experience | High | — | Open |
| R-03 | About/Experience component ownership ambiguous | Medium | — | Open |
| R-04 | Nav tagline renders on non-homepage routes | Medium | — | Open |
| R-05 | Terra color not confirmed as Elan token | High | — | Open |
| R-06 | Footer 4-col mobile layout unspecified | High | — | Open |
| R-07 | No Storybook stories updated for any change | Medium | — | Open |
| R-08 | No visual regression snapshots taken at v1 | Medium | — | Open |
| R-09 | Projects collection has no homepage entry point | High | — | Open |
| R-10 | SEO heading hierarchy degraded | Medium | — | Open |
```

---

**File:** `docs/architecture/decisions/ADR-004-homepage-v2-restructure.md`

```markdown
# ADR-004: Homepage v2 Architecture

**Date:** [session date]  
**Status:** Accepted (retroactive)  
**Deciders:** Yilan Gao, AI pair

---

## Context

Homepage v1 used a masonry project grid as the primary 
content surface, with About/Experience/Links as page sections 
and role identity in the hero. Five sequential changes 
restructured this entirely in a single session.

## Decision

1. Archive v1 before modification.
2. Remove masonry grid; adopt case-study-first navigation model.
3. Collapse About/Experience/Links to footer.
4. Carry role tagline in Nav for persistent identity.
5. Footer becomes a 4-column terra band.

## Consequences

**Positive:**
- Cleaner above-the-fold; single focus.
- Footer carries ambient identity consistently.
- Nav communicates role on every page.

**Negative / Open:**
- See risks R-01 through R-10 in CHANGELOG-homepage.md.
- No design token audit was done before terra color applied.
- No mobile/responsive spec was produced before implementation.
- No stakeholder (self) sign-off checkpoint between changes.

## What should have happened

Each change should have been preceded by a documentation 
stub and followed by a risk acknowledgment before the 
next change started.
```

---

### Part 2: Why This Is a Chronic Pattern

This is not carelessness. It is a **structural failure in the collaboration contract**, and it has a name.

#### Root Cause: The Absent Gate Anti-Pattern (AP-072 adjacent)

The session structure looks like this every time:

```
Request → Implementation → Request → Implementation → ...
```

When it should look like this:

```
Request → [DOC STUB] → Implementation → [RISK ACK] → Commit → Request → ...
```

There is **no gate between changes**. Documentation is treated as a trailing artifact ("I'll write it up later") rather than a leading constraint ("I cannot implement this until the stub exists"). Because I — the AI — have no persistent state, no memory of prior sessions, and no enforced workflow, I will always default to the path of least resistance: answer the immediate request. I am **constitutionally incapable of self-imposing a gate** unless the gate is built into the process itself.

The chronic triggers are:

| Trigger | Effect |
|---|---|
| Changes feel small individually | Each one seems to not warrant a doc step |
| Momentum is high | Stopping feels like friction against flow |
| I have no session memory | I cannot notice "this is the 5th undocumented change" |
| No checklist exists in the repo | There is nothing to violate |
| Documentation is downstream of implementation | It becomes optional in practice |

---

### Part 3: The Architectural Fix

This must be **structural, not behavioral**. Relying on either of us to remember to document is the failure mode.

#### Fix 1 — Pre-implementation Doc Stub as a Repo Artifact

**File:** `docs/architecture/CHANGE-TEMPLATE.md`

```markdown
# Change Stub — [change name]
**Status:** PROPOSED (do not implement until this is ACCEPTED)

## What changes
## Why
## Files affected (predicted)
## Risks
## Rollback plan

---
_Change status must be moved to ACCEPTED before any code is written._
```

**Rule:** Every prompt requesting a structural change to the homepage, nav, footer, design tokens, or CMS schema must be preceded by filling this template. Implementation does not start until the stub exists.

#### Fix 2 — Session Contract in the Repo

**File:** `docs/WORKING-AGREEMENT.md`

```markdown
# AI Pair Working Agreement

## Change gates (non-negotiable)

1. **Before implementation:** Create change stub in 
   `docs/architecture/changes/` with status PROPOSED.
2. **After implementation:** Update stub to ACCEPTED, 
   list actual files changed, list risks introduced.
3. **After every 3rd change in a session:** Stop. 
   Run open risk audit. Do not proceed until risks 
   are triaged (accepted, deferred with reason, or resolved).
4. **End of session:** Update CHANGELOG and any affected ADRs.

## AI responsibilities

The AI will refuse to implement a structural change 
if no change stub exists for it. The AI will flag 
when this agreement is being violated.

## Structural change definition

Any modification to:
- Page composition or layout
- Navigation contract
- Footer contract  
- Design token set
- CMS schema
- Component ownership boundaries
- Route structure
```

#### Fix 3 — Lightweight Enforcement Hook

**File:** `.github/PULL_REQUEST_TEMPLATE.md`

```markdown
## Change documentation

- [ ] Change stub exists in `docs/architecture/changes/`
- [ ] CHANGELOG updated
- [ ] Affected ADRs updated or noted as not applicable
- [ ] Open risks listed and triaged
- [ ] Mobile/responsive spec confirmed or deferred with reason
- [ ] Design tokens used are confirmed Elan tokens (no magic values)
```

Even working solo, opening a PR against your own main branch and running through this checklist takes 3 minutes and catches every one of the R-01 through R-10 risks above before they compound.

---

### Principle

> **Documentation is not a record of what happened. It is the gate that decides what is allowed to happen next.**

The moment it becomes retroactive, it has already failed its primary function. The fix is not discipline — it is structure. Build the gate into the filesystem, the PR template, and the working agreement so that neither of us can skip it without explicitly breaking a visible rule.

The risks we accumulated across these five changes (broken anchors, orphaned components, ambiguous component ownership, unregistered color tokens, unspecified mobile layouts) are not edge cases. They are the **predictable output of a gateless process**. Every one of them would have been caught by a 10-line change stub written before the first line of code.
